title: 帷幕
summary:
While observing Daisy Lettrél gardening, Hastings experiences feelings of resentment toward her. Nearby, Norton, Boyd Carrington, and Colonel Lettrél engage in conversation. Carrington lightens the mood with an exaggerated story about an Irish sergeant who accidentally killed his brother while hunting. Following the story, Norton praises Carrington’s success, prompting Lettrél to suggest that such success is often the result of luck rather than merit. The atmosphere becomes awkward when Norton makes a clumsy comment regarding a wife bullying her husband, which Lettrél defends in a stern, unemotional manner.

The tension is interrupted when Lettrél attempts to shoot a pigeon but misses. He then spots movement in the tall grass, which he believes to be a rabbit eating saplings. He fires his gun, but the shot is followed by a woman's scream and a gurgling sound. Hastings discovers that Lettrél has accidentally shot Daisy in the shoulder while she was securing saplings to stakes. A nauseated Norton runs to fetch medical help, and Nurse Craven and Dr. Franklin arrive to treat her. Daisy is moved inside to a bed, and Dr. Franklin informs Hastings that the bullet missed her vital organs.

A dazed and distraught Lettrél is later found in the smoking room, claiming he mistook a reflection for a rabbit. When Hastings discusses the incident with Judith and Alton, Alton reacts with callousness, suggesting the shooting might have been a "nice way out" for the Colonel or even intentional. This cynicism causes Hastings to doubt the accidental nature of the event. Later, Hastings and Carrington reflect on the possibility that Daisy may have provoked Lettrél.
